articles.title: 7 players Bruins could target at 2026 NHL trade deadline

summary: Bruins plan to leverage draft picks for potential trades by 2026 NHL deadline.
Targeting players with term or high upside could enhance team performance.
Local fans may see roster changes aimed at improving playoff chances.

articles.title: Staff member at special needs residential program in Franklin accused of breaking autistic student’s arm
articles.description:
The man was working at a Franklin residential program for people with disabilities when he allegedly broke the student’s arm.

summary: Staff member at Franklin program accused of breaking an autistic student's arm.

articles.title: ‘Every run is about defying cancer’: This 18-year-old is running Boston for her childhood friend
articles.description:
"The long runs teach you how to stay in discomfort without panicking."

The post ‘Every run is about defying cancer’: This 18-year-old is running Boston for her childhood friend appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: 18-year-old Amanda Lewis runs the Boston Marathon to honor her childhood friend battling cancer.
She trains to cope with discomfort, reflecting her commitment to the cause.
Her efforts raise awareness and support for cancer research at Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.

articles.title: What’s next for Patriots’ offense after releasing Stefon Diggs?

articles.title: Each mile is for her miracle: This Granby mom is running the Boston Marathon with her daughter in mind
articles.description:
"With every mile I run, I will be thinking of her strength, her transplant journey, and the families who are walking similar paths right now."

The post Each mile is for her miracle: This Granby mom is running the Boston Marathon with her daughter in mind appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Granby mom Brianna Poehler runs the Boston Marathon for her daughter’s transplant journey.
She aims to raise awareness for families facing similar medical challenges.
The marathon takes place on April 17, 2023, impacting local community support.
